name: Understanding easing and cubic-bezier curves in CSS
snippet:
The easing of any web-based movement is what's called a cubic bézier curve. it means is: a line, usually curved, defined by four numeric values ...
The easing curve can make or break any animation on the web. Let's look at the science of CSS cubic-bezier curves, and the art of using them ...

name: Understanding the Cubic Bézier Curves - aworkinprogress.dev
snippet: A cubic Bézier curve is a [0,1] curve with at most two smooth turns. With CSS, a cubic Bézier curve is defined by four points.

name: Crafting Easing Curves for User Interfaces | by Ryan Brownhill
snippet:
An easing curve is a line that defines the acceleration pattern on a graph. Easing curves are commonly referred by many other names such as Motion Curves, ...
Easing curves are commonly referred by many other names such as Motion Curves, Timing Functions, Bezier Curves or just Curves.

name: cubic-bezier() - CSS-Tricks
snippet: The cubic-bezier() function lets you define custom timing curves for CSS animations and transitions. It gives you precise control over how elements move.

name: cubic-bezier() CSS function - MDN Web Docs
snippet:
A cubic Bézier curve is defined by four points: P0, P1, P2, and P3. In animations, this creates a kind of "bouncing" effect.
The cubic Bézier functions, often called "smooth" easing functions, In animations, this creates a kind of "bouncing" effect. Examples Bouncing ...
